case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for acetaldehyde on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Best-available reconstruction from sanitized filename CC_O and formula C2H4O; large-adsorbate ablation candidate

plan.reasoning: History shows all major high-symmetry sites tested with progressive stabilization; best is hollow (Hf,Zn,Zn) at -0.870 eV via O (index 2). No convergence tag present and energies still differ, but further improvement possible on chemically distinct Zn-rich hollow. New plan: hollow Zn-Zn-Zn via O (index 2). Predicted MACE-MP-0 energy for this unexplored environment is modestly lower.

History is empty so this is the first attempt. SMILES CC=O matches acetaldehyde with O (index 2) as the logical donor atom for intact molecular adsorption via lone pair. Propose initial ontop-Zn site as a standard starting point on the mixed Zn/Hf surface.

No Hf sites or hollows tested yet; surface heterogeneity suggests possible improvement on mixed Hf-Zn environments. Propose new bridge Hf-Zn plan with O (index 2). Predicted MACE energy slightly better than current best due to Hf oxophilicity.

plan.reasoning: History tested ontop Zn (E=-0.75 eV), bridge Zn-Zn (E=-0.81 eV) and bridge Hf-Zn (E=-0.83 eV), all binding via O (index 2). All succeeded without dissociation. To seek lower energy, explore a new hollow site using the available Hf-Zn-Zn combination while keeping single-point O binding. This is chemically distinct from prior bridges.
